Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2022-02-11 section=business period_end=2021-12-31
Item 1.    Business
General
Verizon Communications Inc. (Verizon or the Company) is a holding company that, acting through its subsidiaries, is one of the world's leading providers of communications, technology, information and entertainment products and services to consumers, businesses and government entities. With a presence around the world, we offer data, video and voice services and solutions on our networks and platforms that are designed to meet customers' demand for mobility, reliable network connectivity, security and control.
Our principal executive offices are located at 1095 Avenue of the Americas, New York, New York 10036 (telephone number 212-395-1000).
We have two reportable segments that we operate and manage as strategic business units - Verizon Consumer Group (Consumer) and Verizon Business Group (Business).
On September 1, 2021, we completed the sale of our media business, Verizon Media Group (Verizon Media), to an affiliate of Apollo Global Management Inc. Additional information is included in Note 3 to the consolidated financial statements of Verizon Communications Inc. and Subsidiaries.
Verizon Consumer Group
Our Consumer segment provides consumer-focused wireless and wireline communications services and products. Our wireless services are provided across one of the most extensive wireless networks in the Unite
…[truncated 20662 chars]…
viding customers with convenient and easy-to-use products and services and focusing on their needs in order to promote long-term relationships and minimize churn.
Customer service is highly valued by our Business customers. We provide Global Enterprise and Public Sector and Other customers with ready access to their system and performance information, and we conduct proactive testing of our networks to identify issues before they affect our customers. We service our Small and Medium Business customers through service representatives and online support, as well as through store-based representatives for small business customers. For Wholesale customers, we pursue service improvement through continued system automation initiatives.
- Product differentiation. Customer and revenue growth are increasingly dependent on the development of new and enhanced products and services, as the delivery of new and innovative products and services has been accelerating. Customers are shifting their focus from access to applications and are seeking ways to leverage their broadband, video and wireless connections. To compete effectively, providers need to continuously review, improve and refine their product portfolio and develop and rapidly deploy new products and services tailored to the needs of customers. We continue to pursue the development and rapid deployment of new and innovative products and services, both independently and in collaboration with application providers, content providers and device manufacturers. Features such as wireless and wireline inter-operability are becoming increasingly important, driven by both customer demand and technological advancement.
- Sales and distribution. A key to achieving sales success in the consumer and small and medium business sectors of the wireless industry is the reach and quality of sales channels and distribution points. We seek to optimally vary distribution channels among our company-operated stores selling wireless products and services, web-based sales and fulfillment capabilities, outside sales teams and telemarketing, our extensive indirect distribution network of retail outlets and our sale of wireless service to resellers, which resell wireless services to their end-users.
In addition to these competitive factors and trends, companies with a global presence are increasingly competing with us in our Business segment. A relatively small number of telecommunications and integrated service providers with global operations serve customers in the global enterprise market and, to a lesser extent, the global wholesale market. We compete with these providers for large contracts to provide integrated solutions to global enterprises and government customers. Many of these companies have strong market presence, brand recognition and existing customer relationships, all of which contribute to intensifying competition that may affect our future revenue growth.
In the Small and Medium Business market, customer purchasing behaviors and preferences continue to evolve. Solution speed and simplicity with user interfaces that have a consumer-like "look and feel" are becoming key differentiators for customers who are seeking full life-cycle offers that simplify the process of starting, running and growing their businesses. Several major cable operators also offer bundles with wireless services through strategic relationships.
In the Global Enterprise and Public Sector and Other markets, competition levels remain high, primarily as a result of increased industry focus on technology convergence. We compete in this area with system integrators, carriers, and hardware and software providers. In addition, some of the largest information technology services companies are making strategic acquisitions, divesting non-strategic assets and forging new alliances to improve their cost structure. Many new alliances and acquisitions have focused on emerging fields, such as cloud computing, software defined networking, communication applications and other computing tasks via networks, rather than by the use of in-house machines.
Our Wholesale business competes with traditional carriers for long-haul, voice and IP services. In addition, mobile video and data needs are driving a greater need for wireless backhaul. Network providers, cable companies and niche players are competitors for this business opportunity.
Global Network and Technology
Our global network architecture is used by Consumer and Business. Our network technology platforms include both wireless and wireline technologies.
Network Evolution
We are transforming the architecture of our networks into our Intelligent Edge Network, providing improved efficiency and virtualization, increased automation and opportunities for edge computing services that will support our fiber-based and radio access network technologies. We expect that this new architecture will simplify operations by eliminating legacy network elements, speed the deployment of 5G wireless technology and create new opportunities in the business market in a cost-efficient manner.
5G Deployment
Over the past several years, we have been leading the development of 5G wireless technology industry standards and the ecosystems for fixed and mobile 5G wireless services. We expect that 5G technology will provide higher throughput and lower latency than the current fourth-generation (4G) LTE technology and enable our networks to handle more traffic as the number of internet-connected devices grows. As of December 31, 2021, 5G Ultra Wideband is available in parts of 87 U.S. cities and 5G Home is available in parts of 65 U.S. cities. Our FWA
8

broadband service continued to grow during the year with a customer base of approximately 223 thousand as of December 31, 2021. In January 2022, we successfully deployed C-Band spectrum, reaching approximately 100 million people in the U.S. as of February 2022.
5G Nationwide uses low and mid-band spectrum and dynamic spectrum sharing (DSS) technology, which allows 5G service to run simultaneously with 4G LTE on multiple spectrum bands. With DSS, whenever customers move outside Verizon's high-band Ultra Wideband coverage area, their 5G-enabled devices will remain on 5G technology using the lower spectrum bands where the 5G Nationwide network is available. This allows us to more fully and effectively utilize our current spectrum resources to serve both 4G and 5G customers.
4G LTE
The wireless network technology platform that carries the majority of our wireless traffic is 4G LTE, which provides higher data throughput performance for data services at a lower cost compared to that offered by 3G technology. As of December 31, 2021, our 4G LTE network covers approximately 328 million people, including those in areas served by our LTE in Rural America partners. Under this program, we have collaborated with wireless carriers in rural areas to build and operate a 4G LTE network using each carrier's network assets with our core 4G LTE equipment and 700 Megahertz (MHz) C Block and Advanced Wireless Services (AWS) spectrum. LTE Home Internet, our home broadband internet service, leverages the Verizon 4G LTE network.
Wireless Network Reliability and Build-Out
We consider the reliability, coverage and speed of our wireless network to be key factors for our continued success. We believe that steady and consistent network and platform investments provide the foundation for innovative products and services. As we design and deploy our network, we focus on the number of successful data sessions the network enables, delivering on our advertised throughput speeds, and the number of calls that are connected on the first attempt and completed without being dropped. VZ.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: VZ (Decision Date: 2022-10-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Latest quarterly revenue (2022-06-30 Q2) was $33.79B. Growth is near-zero: QoQ +0.7%, YoY +0.1%. TTM revenue is $134.32B.
*   **Earnings & Margins:** Latest quarterly net income was $5.32B, EPS diluted $1.24. QoQ EPS growth was +13.8%, but YoY EPS declined -11.4%. TTM net income is $21.32B. Latest net margin is 0.159, operating margin is 0.237.
*   **Cash Flow:** **Data unavailable** for all reported periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew from $296.99B (Q3 2020) to $370.15B (Q2 2022). Total equity grew from $66.53B to $87.36B over the same period.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** **Cash flow data unavailable,** precluding assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion. Reported net income and operating income show volatility relative to stable revenue.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** High leverage is indicated. For Q2 2022, total liabilities of $282.79B represent ~76% of total assets ($370.15B), yielding a high liability-to-equity ratio of ~3.24x. Equity has grown from the prior quarter ($85.17B to $87.36B).

**3. Valuation vs. History**
The stock is trading at historically low multiples. As of 2022-09-30:
*   P/E (diluted) is 7.485, below the 1-year window range of 7.5 to 11.0.
*   Price-to-Book is 1.826, below the 1-year window range of 1.8 to 3.0 and down sharply from 2.995 one year prior (2021-10-01).
*   Price-to-Sales is 1.188.
*   Earnings Yield is 0.134 (13.4%).

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Level:** The latest close (2022-09-30) is $37.97, at the bottom of the ~1-year range ($37.95 - $55.51).
*   **Returns:** Price performance has been strongly negative relative to fundamentals. The quarterly return (from 2022-07-01) is -26.5%. The annual return (from 2021-09-30) is -29.7%.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The primary quantitative signal is extreme valuation compression. A reversion to the midpoint of its 1-year P/E range (~9.25x) on current TTM EPS of ~$4.99 implies significant price appreciation from current levels.
*   **Downside:** Key risks are 1) Stagnant top-line growth (YoY revenue +0.1%) limiting earnings expansion, 2) High financial leverage (liabilities/equity ~3.24x), and 3) The complete lack of cash flow data to confirm earnings quality and debt service capacity.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
VZ presents a classic value profile: extreme valuation compression (P/E 7.5x, P/B 1.83x) coinciding with a near-30% annual price decline, against a backdrop of flat revenue growth but stable reported margins. The balance sheet carries high leverage. The absence of cash flow data is a critical gap, preventing a full assessment of financial resilience and true cash-generative power. The risk/reward is framed by the deep discount to historical multiples versus fundamental stagnation and leverage.
VZ.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment Report: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ)
**Decision Date:** 2022-10-01

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative
Verizon is one of the world’s leading providers of communications, technology, and entertainment products and services. It operates through two primary segments: **Verizon Consumer Group** and **Verizon Business Group**. The Consumer segment provides wireless and wireline services (including Fios fiber-optic) to approximately 115 million wireless retail connections and serves nine states in the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast U.S. The Business segment serves businesses and government entities with a portfolio of network, security, IoT, and professional services, accounting for approximately 27 million wireless retail postpaid connections. The company emphasizes network reliability, 5G leadership, and fiber-optic expansion as key differentiators. The 2021 acquisition of TracFone significantly expanded its prepaid wireless offerings.

#### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **Corporate Actions & Community Response (2022-09-30):** Verizon expanded unlimited talk/text/data relief to postpaid and prepaid customers in areas impacted by Hurricane Ian and pledged a $125,000 donation to the Florida Disaster Fund.
*   **Product & Platform Development (2022-09-28):** Verizon brought the NFL+ streaming service to its "+play" platform, which allows customers to manage subscriptions, and unveiled new 5G home entertainment advancements at Mobile World Congress.
*   **Strategic Outlook (2022-09-06, 2022-08-12):** A Bloomberg report noted Verizon attracts investors due to ongoing 5G subscription growth and new opportunities in fiber and fixed broadband. The new head of Verizon Business highlighted trends in mobility, broadband, and cloud, aiming to expand total addressable market by $90 billion.
*   **Market Sentiment (2022-09-30):** Telecommunications stocks, including Verizon, posted their steepest quarterly declines in two decades.
*   **Analyst Sentiment (2022-09-29):** Over the past three months, 13 analysts rated Verizon. The majority (9 of 13) were "Indifferent." The average price target was $52.23 versus a stock price of $38.92.

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Intense Competition & Commoditization:** The 10-K and a Sep 16, 2022 Reddit post describe severe competitive pressures from AT&T, T-Mobile, cable companies (e.g., bundling wireless), DISH Network's build-out, and non-traditional OTT providers. The industry faces "downward pricing pressure," especially on wired services.
    *   **Legal and Regulatory Exposure:** A Sep 7, 2022 lawsuit alleges Verizon failed to disconnect customers engaged in piracy. A Sep 27, 2022 settlement of $3.2 million was reached for alleged overtime pay violations. Regulatory changes could also impact operations.
    *   **Macroeconomic & Sector Pressure:** The Sep 30, 2022 report notes the sector experienced its worst quarterly decline in two decades, indicating significant investor concern.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Network and Scale Leadership:** The company's extensive 4G LTE coverage, accelerating 5G Ultra Wideband/C-Band deployment, and fiber-optic network (Fios) provide a foundation for growth and service differentiation.
    *   **Strategic Growth Platforms:** Initiatives like the "+play" content aggregation platform (Sep 28, 2022) and targeted growth in 5G fixed wireless access (FWA), fiber, and enterprise mobility/broadband/cloud services aim to expand the addressable market (Aug 12, 2022).

#### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** The franchise holds a strong foundational position in wireless and wireline networks, with clear strategic initiatives targeting growth in 5G, fiber, and connected services. However, the near-term narrative is dominated by intense commoditizing competition, ongoing legal risks, and recent severe sector-wide stock performance, which have overshadowed long-term catalysts. Analyst sentiment is overwhelmingly indifferent. The outlook is balanced between solid strategic positioning and significant headwinds.

corrections.issue: Quant report states P/E of 7.485 is 'below the 1-year window range of 7.5 to 11.0' — the raw data shows the range as 7.5..11.0, and the latest P/E of 7.485 is technically below the stated lower bound. This is a minor inconsistency in framing (the report says 'below' which is correct, but the range lower bound of 7.5 is very close to 7.485, making this essentially at the floor rather than meaningfully below it). No material issue.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; the statement is technically accurate.

corrections.issue: Quant report claims TTM EPS of ~$4.99. The raw data shows quarterly EPS of 1.24 + 1.09 + 1.11 + 1.55 = 4.99, which is consistent. However, the report also states 'current TTM EPS of ~$4.99' in the upside scenario. The raw data does not provide a TTM EPS figure directly, but the sum of the last four quarters' EPS (1.24+1.09+1.11+1.55) equals 4.99, so this is derivable and correct.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; the figure is derivable from the quarterly EPS data provided.

corrections.issue: Qual report references a '$90 billion' TAM expansion figure attributed to Verizon Business head (Aug 12, 2022). The raw qualitative evidence confirms this: 'Sowmyanarayan Sampath... can grow total addressable market by $90 billion.' This is supported.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; claim is supported by the raw evidence.

**Decision Date: 2022-10-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**  
- **Revenue**: Latest Q2 (2022-07-31) revenue = $152.86B, +8.0% QoQ, +8.4% YoY. TTM revenue = $587.82B.  
- **Earnings**: Q2 EPS diluted = $1.88, +154.1% QoQ, +23.7% YoY. TTM net income = $14.02B.  
- **Margins**: Q2 gross margin = 24.2% ($37.02B/$152.86B), operating margin = 4.5% ($6.85B/$152.86B), net margin = 3.4% ($5.15B/$152.86B). TTM net margin = 2.4%.  
- **Cash Flow**: No supplied cash flow data.  
- **Balance Sheet**: Latest total equity = $85.59B, stable QoQ. Total debt as of 2022-01-31 = $37.67B; debt-to-equity ≈ 0.44.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings Quality**: Volatile; net income ranged from -$2.01B (2021-01-31) to $5.20B (2020-10-31) over the 8-quarter sample. Q2 net margin (3.4%) is below Q2 2021 level (4.6%).  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity stable around $85–86B over recent quarters. Debt-to-equity ratio low (0.44). Liabilities consistently reported; no signs of distress.

**3. Valuation Versus History**  
- **Current Valuation (2022-09-30)**: P/E diluted = 8.468, P/B = 1.387, P/S = 0.202, earnings yield = 11.8%.  
- **Historical Range (2021-10 to 2022-09)**: P/E diluted range = 8.1–16.4; current P/E is near the low end. P/B range = 1.2–1.6; current is mid-range.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**  
- **Price (2022-09-30)**: Close = $43.23; 52-week range = $39.09–$53.59.  
- **Returns**: Annual return (since 2021-09-30) = -6.9%; quarterly return (since 2022-07-01) = +5.8%.  
- **Fundamental Link**: Price is down annually despite TTM revenue growth (+8.4% YoY), reflecting thin margins (net margin 2.4% TTM) and earnings volatility.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**  
- **Upside**: Valuation re-rating if earnings stabilize—P/E could expand toward the historical median of ~14x. Current earnings yield (11.8%) exceeds dividend yields of many peers.  
- **Downside**: Margin compression—operating margin (4.5%) is low; further deterioration could pressure earnings. No cash flow data limits assessment of cash generation durability.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
Valuation is at historical lows (P/E 8.47 vs. range 8.1–16.4) but justified by weak earnings quality (volatile net income, thin margins). Balance sheet resilience provides downside protection (debt-to-equity 0.44). Price has underperformed fundamentals over the year (-6.9% annual return vs. +8.4% revenue growth), suggesting market skepticism on margin sustainability. Risk/reward is neutral: potential for multiple expansion if margins improve, but limited catalyst without demonstrated earnings durability.
WMT.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: WMT (as of 2022-10-01)**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the supplied narrative, Walmart (WMT) is characterized as the nation's largest retailer, whose performance is viewed as a barometer for the overall U.S. economy. The company's strategic direction, as indicated by CEO Doug McMillon, involves bulking up its digital distribution channel and expanding into international markets (2022-08-16). Operational investment is evident in its logistics capabilities, such as opening a new, high-tech fulfillment center (2022-09-29).

**2. Recent Developments**
*   **Corporate Actions & Operations (2022-09-29):** Walmart opened a 1.1 million-square-foot "Next Generation" fulfillment center in Joliet, IL, ahead of the holiday season, signaling investment in e-commerce logistics.
*   **Earnings & Guidance (2022-08-16):** Walmart's fiscal second-quarter results beat analyst expectations for earnings and revenue. The company kept its outlook for the second half of the year unchanged.
*   **Inventory Management (2022-08-17):** CFO John David Rainey stated that Walmart had canceled "billions of dollars" in orders and that inventory levels had peaked in the last quarter.
*   **Consumer & Macro Environment (2022-09-28, 2022-09-27):** News highlighted potential underwhelming holiday demand and broader macroeconomic uncertainty. A sector note (2022-09-27) indicated retailers, including Macy's and Dick's, were trimming holiday hiring plans.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal & Regulatory Challenges:** Multiple lawsuits pose potential legal and reputational risk. These include a federal lawsuit related to opioid prescriptions (2022-09-06), an FTC lawsuit over money transfer practices (2022-08-29), and a class action alleging improper collection of biometric data (2022-09-13).
*   **Risk: Demand Softness:** Narrative mentions point to softening demand and macroeconomic challenges, particularly for lower-income consumers (2022-09-30), and concerns about the holiday shopping season (2022-09-28).
*   **Risk: Sector Headwinds:** Broader retail sector pressures, including inventory bloat and hiring pullbacks, could indicate an industry-wide slowdown (2022-09-27).
*   **Offset: Demonstrated Operational Resilience:** Recent quarterly earnings showed resilience, with sales rising even as consumers cut back on discretionary items (2022-08-16). Proactive inventory actions (2022-08-17) demonstrate management responsiveness.
*   **Offset: Strategic Investment:** Continued investment in digital and fulfillment infrastructure (2022-09-29) supports long-term competitiveness.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** The evidence presents a mixed picture. Confirmed positive developments include a recent earnings beat, maintained guidance, and strategic operational investments in e-commerce infrastructure. These are offset by confirmed significant legal risks from multiple lawsuits and inferred risks of consumer demand softness and sectoral headwinds. The company's large scale provides resilience, but near-term pressures are evident.

The raw valuation ratios show net_margin=0.024 for the latest period (2022-09-30), which is 2.4%. The Q2 quarterly net margin of 3.4% is arithmetically correct from the income statement, but the report's framing could confuse the quarterly margin with the TTM/valuation-ratio margin. No material inconsistency, but the report should clarify which margin figure maps to the valuation ratio.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Total debt as of 2022-01-31 = $37.67B; debt-to-equity ≈ 0.44.' The raw balance sheet only provides total_debt for 2022-01-31 ($37.67B) and total_equity for 2022-07-31 ($85.59B). The D/E ratio of 0.44 mixes a Jan 2022 debt figure with a Jul 2022 equity figure. Using consistent 2022-01-31 figures: 37.67B/91.89B = 0.41. The report should note the date mismatch or use the consistent ratio.
corrections.required_action: Acknowledge the date mismatch in the D/E calculation. The ratio is approximately 0.41 using consistent 2022-01-31 data, not 0.44.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Walmart trades at a historically low P/E of ~8.5x with a strong balance sheet (D/E ~0.41) and resilient revenue growth (+8.4% YoY), but thin and volatile margins, multiple legal overhangs, and macro headwinds limit conviction in a near-term re-rating. The risk/reward skews mildly positive but lacks a clear catalyst.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Walmart trades at a historically low P/E of ~8.5x with a strong balance sheet (D/E ~0.41) and resilient revenue growth (+8.4% YoY), but thin and volatile margins, multiple legal overhangs, and macro headwinds limit conviction in a near-term re-rating. The risk/reward skews mildly positive but lacks a clear catalyst.
key_factors: Valuation at historical lows: P/E of 8.47x sits near the bottom of the 8.1–16.4x range over the past year, with an earnings yield of 11.8% that exceeds many peers, offering a margin of safety if earnings stabilize.

Revenue resilience amid macro uncertainty: TTM revenue of $587.82B grew +8.4% YoY, and Q2 2022 revenue of $152.86B beat expectations, demonstrating Walmart's defensive positioning as the nation's largest retailer.

Thin and volatile earnings quality: TTM net margin is just 2.4%, with quarterly net income ranging from -$2.01B to $5.20B over the trailing eight quarters; the corrected Q2 2022 net margin (3.4%) is only modestly above Q2 2021 (~3.1%), not meaningfully improving.

Multiple legal and macro headwinds: Opioid-related federal lawsuit (2022-09-06), FTC money-transfer suit (2022-08-29), biometric data class action (2022-09-13), and softening consumer demand with retailers trimming holiday hiring plans (2022-09-27) create near-term uncertainty.

  filing_date=2022-08-05 section=business period_end=2022-06-30
Item 1.  Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is focused on providing branded products of superior quality and value to improve the lives of the world's consumers, now and for generations to come.  The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.  Today, our products are sold in approximately 180 countries and territories.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.  Unless the context indicates otherwise, the terms the "Company," "P&G," "we," "our" or "us" as used herein refer to The Procter & Gamble Company (the registrant) and its subsidiaries.
Throughout this Form 10-K, we incorporate by reference information from other documents filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K, and amendments thereto, are filed electronically with the SEC.  The SEC maintains an internet site that contains these reports at: www.sec.gov.  Reports can also be accessed through links from our website at: www.pginvestor.com.  P&G includes the website link solely as a textual reference.  The information contained on our website is not incorporated by reference into this report.
Copies of these reports are also available, without charge, by contacting EQ Shareowner Services, 1100 Centre Pointe Curve, Suite 101, Mendota, MN 55120-4100.
Financial Information about Segments
Information about our reportable segments can be found in the MD&A and Note 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Narrative Description of Business
Business Model
.  Our business model relies on the continued growth and success of existing brands and products, as well as the creation of new innovative products and brands.  The markets and industry segments in which we offer our products are highly competitive.  Our products are sold in approximately 180 countries and territories through numerous channels as well as direct-to-consumer.  Our growth strategy is to deliver meaningful and noticeable superiority across five key vectors of our consumer proposition - product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution and consumer and customer value.  We use our research and development (R&D) and consumer insights to provide superior products and packaging.  We utilize our marketing and online presence to deliver superior brand messaging to our consumers.  We work collaboratively with our customers to deliver superior retail execution, both in-store and o
…[truncated 2028 chars]…
 these patents or licenses cover significant product formulation and processes used to manufacture our products.  The trademarks are important to the overall marketing and branding of our products.  All major trademarks in each business are registered.  In part, our success can be attributed to the existence and continued protection of these trademarks, patents and licenses.
Competitive Condition
.  The markets in which our products are sold are highly competitive.  Our products compete against similar products of many large and small companies, including well-known global competitors.  In many of the markets and industry segments in which we sell our products, we compete against other branded products as well as retailers' private-label brands.  We are well positioned in the industry segments and markets in which we operate, often holding a leadership or significant market share position.  We support our products with advertising, promotions and other marketing vehicles to build awareness
2        The Procter & Gamble Company
and trial of our brands and products in conjunction with our sales force.  We believe this combination provides the most efficient method of marketing for these types of products.  Product quality, performance, value and packaging are also important differentiating factors.
Government Regulation
.  Our Company is subject to a wide variety of laws and regulations across the countries in which we do business.  In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC).  We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree.  We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and data privacy and protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.  For additional information on the potential impacts of global legal and regulatory requirements on our business, see "Item 1A. Risk Factors" herein.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations.  We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance.  We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2023 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
.  Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage.  Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business.  We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategies relating to talent including diversity, equality and inclusion as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices.  The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2022, the Company had approximately 106,000 employees, an increase of five percent versus the prior year due primarily to business growth.  The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures.  49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 26% of our employees are located in the United States.  41% of our global employees are women.  As of June 30, 2022, 28% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining skilled and diverse talent, both from universities and the broader market.  We recruit from among the best universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent.  We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities.  Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Diversity, Equality and Inclusion
As a consumer products company, we believe that it is important for our workforce to reflect the diversity of our consumers worldwide.  We also seek to foster an inclusive work environment where each individual can bring their authentic self, which helps drive innovation and enables us to better serve our consumers.  We aspire to achieve equal gender representation globally and at key management and leadership levels.  Within the U.S. workforce, our aspiration is to achieve 40% multicultural representation overall as well as at management and leadership levels.
Compensation and Benefits
Our compensation plans are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent and in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs.  We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance in line with local country practices with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
.  Environmental sustainability is a key focus area and integrated into P&G's business strategies.  The Company has declared its focus on developing irresistibly superior products and packages that are sustainable.  The Company announced an ambition to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, purchase renewable electricity for our operations, reduce our use of virgin petroleum-based plastic in our packaging, increase the recyclability or reusability of our packaging and increase responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities such as wood pulp and palm oil.
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts including our TCFD (Task Force on Climate-Related Financial Disclosures), SASB (Sustainability Accounting Standards Board) and CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) reports can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.  References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K.
PG.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: Procter & Gamble (PG) as of 2022-10-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory (latest period: Q4 2022, ending 2022-06-30)**
- Revenue: $19.52B (QoQ +0.7%, YoY +3.0%). TTM revenue: $80.19B.
- EPS diluted: $1.22 (QoQ -8.3%, YoY +8.0%). TTM net income: $14.79B.
- Gross margin (TTM): 47.4% ($38.03B/$80.19B). Operating margin (TTM): 22.2% ($17.81B/$80.19B). Net margin (TTM): 18.4% ($14.79B/$80.19B).
- Balance sheet: total assets $117.21B, total equity $46.85B, debt-to-equity 1.50x ($70.35B/$46.85B). Equity stable between $44.89B and $48.58B over eight quarters.
- Cash flow: unavailable.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- Earnings quality: TTM ROE 31.6%; stable TTM net margin at 18.4%. Cash flow data missing to assess accruals.
- Balance-sheet resilience: equity base ~$47B; liabilities decreased from $76.52B (2021-12-31) to $70.35B (2022-06-30). Leverage moderate at 1.50x.

**3. Valuation vs. Own History**
- Latest PE diluted: 21.73 (vs. 1-year range 21.7–29.7). Latest P/B: 6.842 (vs. 1-year range 6.8–9.3). Both at low end of ranges.
- Earnings yield: 4.6%. Net margin and ROE stable at ~18.4% and ~31.6% over the period.

**4. Price and Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
- Price: $126.25 (2022-09-30), near 52-week low of $126.21, below 52-week high of $165.35.
- Quarterly return: -13.6% (from $146.11). Annual return: -9.7% (from $139.80).
- Fundamental path: TTM revenue +3.0% YoY, EPS +8.0% YoY, but latest quarter EPS down QoQ. Price decline contrasts with still-positive TTM growth.

**5. Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- Upside: PE reversion to 1-year midpoint ~25.0 implies price ~$145 (using TTM EPS ~$5.81), +15% from current.
- Downside: Continued earnings pressure (latest quarter EPS down 8.3% QoQ) could sustain low valuation; no cash flow data limits quality assessment.

**6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- Quantitative risk/reward moderately positive: valuation at historical low end with stable margins and high ROE, but missing cash flow data and recent earnings deceleration temper conviction. Upside from mean revaluation, downside from further fundamental weakening.
PG.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: Procter & Gamble (PG)**
**Decision Date:** 2022-10-01 (All evidence strictly point-in-time prior to this date)

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Procter & Gamble is a global consumer staples company founded in 1837, with products sold in approximately 180 countries. Its business model is centered on providing branded products of superior quality and value, driving growth through innovation in existing brands and the creation of new ones. The company's growth strategy is built on achieving "meaningful and noticeable superiority" across five vectors: product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution, and consumer/customer value. It competes in highly competitive markets against both global branded competitors and retailer private-label brands, often holding leadership or significant market share. The company operates a diversified portfolio, with sales to Walmart Inc. representing approximately 15% of total sales, and its top ten customers accounting for about 39% of sales (2022). It emphasizes research and development, marketing, and productivity improvement to drive balanced top- and bottom-line growth. (Source: 10-K Business Section, filed 2022-08-05)

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments
*   **Corporate Governance/Activism (2022-09-20):** Environmental groups have asked Procter & Gamble investors to vote against the re-election of the company's CEO as board chair. The groups argued that the CEO's role overseeing environmental concerns, including forestry, has not succeeded in mitigating risks in P&G's forest sourcing. (Source: Reuters article)
*   **Market Sentiment & Valuation Commentary (Multiple dates in Sept. 2022):** Recent news articles framed PG as a "safe stock" for volatile markets and a defensive holding, noting its outperformance of the S&P 500 since 2017. Commentary also acknowledged some "business headwinds" the company faces. (Source: News summaries from 2022-09-29, 2022-09-25, 2022-09-30)
*   **Earnings Outlook (2022-09-14):** A web snippet cited a Zacks consensus estimate for quarterly revenue of $20.56 billion, up 1.11% year-over-year, and full-year earnings of $5.94 per share. (Source: finance.yahoo.com article)

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **ESG and Activist Pressure:** The company faces direct pressure from environmental investors regarding its forestry sourcing practices, which has escalated to a challenge against the CEO's board leadership. (Source: Reuters, 2022-09-20)
*   **Regulatory and Legal Environment:** P&G is subject to extensive global regulations (FDA, EPA, FTC, etc.), including expanding environmental and sustainability-related laws. The 10-K notes compliance requires ongoing expenditures, though not expected to be material in the near term. A separate source notes past legal scrutiny over chemical levels in products like Tide. (Source: 10-K Business Section; napolilaw.com article, 2022-08-19)
*   **Competitive and Inflationary Pressures:** The markets are described as "highly competitive," and the company's supply chain is exposed to fluctuating prices for commodities, raw materials, and fuel, which it may not always pass to customers. (Source: 10-K Business Section)

**Offsets:**
*   **Strong Brand Portfolio and Market Position:** The company's strategy is built on brand superiority, and it maintains leadership positions in its segments, providing resilience. Its diversified geographic and product portfolio helps mitigate risk. (Source: 10-K Business Section)
*   **Commitment to Compliance and Sustainability:** P&G states it has compliance programs in place and has integrated environmental sustainability into its business strategies, with announced ambitions on emissions and packaging. (Source: 10-K Business Section)
*   **Defensive Consumer Staples Profile:** Market commentary consistently highlights PG's defensive characteristics and stability, which are attractive during economic volatility. (Source: News summaries, Sept. 2022)

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** Procter & Gamble's core franchise remains robust, supported by a global portfolio of leading brands and a detailed strategy for superior execution. However, the recent period has introduced a clear external risk in the form of escalated ESG activism directly targeting the company's leadership. While the fundamental business model appears resilient against typical cyclical and competitive pressures, this new and pointed governance challenge represents a qualitative headwind to monitor, creating a balanced view for the near term.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Equity stable between $44.89B and $48.58B over eight quarters' but the raw balance sheet shows equity ranging from $44.89B to $48.58B — this is correct. However, the report also says 'liabilities decreased from $76.52B (2021-12-31) to $70.35B (2022-06-30)' which is accurate per the raw data. No material issue here.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; claim is supported by raw data.

corrections.issue: The qual report references a Zacks consensus estimate for 'full-year earnings of $5.94 per share' from a 2022-09-14 source. The raw evidence confirms this figure appears in the web context. However, the quant report's TTM EPS can be derived as ~$5.82 ($14.79B net income / ~2.54B shares implied by quarterly EPS figures). The $5.94 figure is a forward consensus, not a trailing actual, and the reports do not conflate these — this is acceptable separation.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; forward estimate and trailing actual are correctly kept distinct.

corrections.issue: The qual report's 'Neutral' assessment is reasonable given the balanced risk/offset structure. However, the ESG activism risk (environmental groups asking investors to vote against CEO re-election as board chair) is described as a 'new and pointed governance challenge' but the raw evidence provides limited detail on the actual scope or likelihood of success of this campaign. The characterization is fair but could be noted as based on a single Reuters article with limited follow-up evidence in the supplied pack.
corrections.required_action: Consider noting that the ESG activism characterization rests on a single source with no subsequent follow-up in the evidence window, which limits assessment of materiality.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: PG trades at the low end of its historical valuation range (PE 21.7 vs. 1-year range 21.7–29.7) with stable margins and high ROE, but recent earnings deceleration, missing cash flow data, and ESG governance headwinds limit conviction in a near-term re-rating.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG trades at the low end of its historical valuation range (PE 21.7 vs. 1-year range 21.7–29.7) with stable margins and high ROE, but recent earnings deceleration, missing cash flow data, and ESG governance headwinds limit conviction in a near-term re-rating.
key_factors: Valuation at historical trough: PE of 21.73 and P/B of 6.842 sit at the bottom of their 1-year ranges, with earnings yield of 4.6%, offering modest upside if mean reversion to ~25x PE materializes (~$145, +15%).

Stable profitability metrics: TTM net margin of 18.4%, ROE of 31.6%, and gross margin of 47.4% remain healthy, supporting the defensive consumer staples thesis even as the stock has declined 9.7% over the past year.

Earnings deceleration risk: Latest quarter EPS declined 8.3% QoQ despite +8.0% YoY growth, and missing cash flow data prevents full earnings quality assessment, tempering confidence in forward estimates of $5.94.

ESG governance headwind: Environmental groups have escalated pressure to vote against CEO re-election as board chair over forestry sourcing (Reuters, 2022-09-20), introducing a qualitative overhang with uncertain materiality.
